sections.heading: Symptoms
sections.content: Vague upper abdominal discomfort: Nonspecific upper abdominal symptoms that may be attributed to benign conditions, contributing to diagnostic delay (Common in early-stage disease). Dyspepsia: Epigastric discomfort, early satiety, or postprandial fullness may be present (Common). Weight loss: Unintentional weight loss is a common presenting symptom, especially in advanced disease (Frequent in advanced disease). Anorexia: Loss of appetite may be an early or late symptom (Common). Dysphagia: Difficulty swallowing, particularly with proximal or EGJ tumors (Variable, more common with proximal tumors). Hematemesis or melena: Acute severe bleeding may occur, directly from the tumor or as a consequence of therapy; patients with acute severe bleeding should undergo prompt endoscopic assessment (Common in advanced disease).
